parks.tagline: A gravity-focused park of technical, rooty and rocky descents — no green flow line, with chicken-ways to bypass the hardest features.
parks.season: May–Oct (summer season; exact dates not published)
parks.blog_intro: Honestly, Wurmberg is not a beginner or little-kid park: its four trails range from a flowy-but-red Freeride line to seriously technical, rooty and rock-strewn descents, and there is no green flow run from the top — every obstacle can be bypassed only via chicken-ways. Confident, gravity-keen older kids who already ride red trails and have full protection will get a buzz from the gondola lift and natural terrain. Families with younger or newer riders may be better served at nearby St. Andreasberg (MSB X-Trail), which a regional bike-park guide highlights for easier starts.

trail_spotlights.name: Enduro
trail_spotlights.note: The most demanding: technical singletrail and root systems; advanced riders only.

parks.tagline: A small Harz bike park with a long blue family flowtrail off the chairlift and a dedicated kids' practice course.
parks.blog_intro: Hahnenklee is a small, friendly Harz bike park with a genuinely gentle blue flowtrail and a dedicated kids' practice course, so a confident young rider who can already control a bike will have plenty to enjoy. The catch is the chairlift: children under roughly 140 cm can't ride it solo and must go up with an adult, and the red and black lines are firmly for older, experienced riders. With kids' rental bikes and on-site coaching, it's a low-pressure place to introduce gravity riding.

trail_spotlights.name: A-Line Flowtrail
trail_spotlights.note: Wide berms and gentle rollers billed as flow for the whole family; the natural first descent for kids and beginners.

trail_spotlights.name: Übungsparcours (practice course)
trail_spotlights.note: Beginner/kids' practice loop ('für Kinder und Fahranfänger'); a good warm-up before the lift.

trail_spotlights.name: Bocksberg Freeride
trail_spotlights.note: Northshore bridges, root passages and small jumps; a step up for confident older kids, not first-timers.

trail_spotlights.name: Hahnenklee Downhill
trail_spotlights.note: Steep sections, rock gardens, jumps and gaps; expert/teen riders only, not for families.

parent_notes: Chairlift: kids may ride alone only from about 140 cm tall ('ab ca. 140 cm allein'); smaller children ride accompanied by an adult. Under-4s ride free and ages 4–14 are discounted.
Full-face helmet is mandatory for all minors, and helmet plus mountain bike are compulsory in the park ('Helm- und Mountainbikepflicht').
Board'n Bikes at the base station rents bikes for adults and children and offers repairs.
Beginner and kids' skills courses run on site; the 'Frechdachsgang' kids' course is roughly ages 4–7 and is only possible with a parent accompanying.
You must push/walk your bike from the lift top to the trail starts — riding on the summit paths is forbidden ('Bis zum Start der Strecken muss das Fahrrad geschoben werden').

parks.name: Black Mountains Bike Park (Dirt Farm Wales)
parks.tagline: A tractor-uplift gravity park built by riders — fast hard-pack, big tables and one of the UK's best jump lines, from blue up to pro.
parks.local_name: Dirt Farm Wales (formerly Black Mountains Cycle Centre)

family_flags.label: Lift carries kids (with guardian on site)

trail_spotlights.name: Berm Baby Berm
trail_spotlights.note: One of five blue "mix-and-match" sections — the most approachable bermy intro at the park, but rated for intermediate riders, not first-timers.

trail_spotlights.name: Rabbit Run
trail_spotlights.note: Flowy blue section — a good confidence-builder for an intermediate young rider stepping up to a gravity park.

trail_spotlights.name: Full Moto
trail_spotlights.note: The pro jump line with two ~40 ft tabletops — strictly for expert riders; a spectator highlight, not for kids.

parent_notes: There are no green/beginner trails — the easiest lines are blue and aimed at intermediate riders with previous off-road experience, so this is not a first-bike park.
Under-18s are welcome but a parent or guardian must stay on site at all times (operator FAQ).
You MUST book online in advance for push-and-ride and uplift; uplift is by tractor and trailer (open to the weather — dress for it).
No bike or body-armour hire on site — bring your own kit; full-face helmet and pads are strongly recommended. Bikes can be hired from Drover Cycles in Hay-on-Wye.
A push-up option (~15–25 min walk) lets you sample the hill cheaply before committing to a full uplift day; a skills area and a pump track are on site to warm up.
No camping or campervans on site — use nearby campsites in the Black Mountains / Brecon Beacons.
parks.blog_intro: Black Mountains Bike Park — now trading as Dirt Farm Wales — is a rider-built, gravity-only hill above Abergavenny. There are no green runs here: the gentlest lines are blue and pitched at intermediate riders, climbing through red and black to a pro jump line with 40-foot tables. For a confident pre-teen who already rides off-road it's a brilliant, friendly step into bike-park gravity (with a parent on site, as the operator requires); for true beginners or little kids it isn't the right first park — a green-graded centre like nearby BikePark Wales will suit them far better.

rows.html: Tractor-and-trailer uplift; or self push-up (~15–25 min walk). Uplift capacity Not found [3] [5]

rows.label: Season & hours
rows.html: Open ~year-round; uplift days Wed–Sun + Bank Holiday Mon (winter days vary — check calendar). Open 10:00–18:00; first uplift 10:00, last uplift 16:00, tracks rideable to 18:00; Thu evening uplift 16:00–20:00. Booking required. [1] [3]

rows.label: Gravity Card
rows.html: No — the Gravity Card network covers Alpine/Central-European parks (DE, AT, CZ, CH, SI, IT, PL) only; no UK parks are listed. [6]

rows.label: Character
rows.html: Rider-built gravity park on a working hill farm — fast hard-packed dirt through woodland and open hillside, with big tables, flowy berms, a dramatic bridge and one of the UK's best jump lines. Designed by Shaun Bevan & Gary Broad. [1] [5]

rows.label: Trail types
rows.html: Downhill / freeride, flow, jump lines, dirt jumps, pump track and a skills section [4] [5]

rows.label: Difficulty spread
rows.html: 556  (17 named "mix-and-match" trail sections: 5 blue / 5 red / 6 black, plus the "Full Moto" pro jump line). No green/beginner trails. [4]

rows.label: Vertical / longest
rows.html: Vertical drop Not found; longest descent Not found (sources describe ~15–25 min push-up to the top) [5]

rows.label: Family friendly
rows.html: Partly — under-18s welcome but a parent/guardian must stay on site at all times; no green trails so not aimed at young children or first-timers. [3]

rows.label: Beginner friendly
rows.html: No — the easiest lines are blue, rated for intermediate riders with previous off-road experience; "not for beginners." [3] [4]

rows.label: Skills area
rows.html: Yes (no belt) — a skills area off the main car park plus a pump track at the top car park; no magic-carpet/conveyor. [5]

rows.label: Standout features
rows.html: "Full Moto" pro jump line with two ~40 ft tabletops (among the biggest jumps in the UK); a dramatic bridge feature, big tables and flowy berms. [4] [5]

rows.label: Bike shop / repair
rows.html: On-site Gateway Cycles pop-up stocks essential spares (tyres, mech hangers); no full repair shop or bike hire on site (hire via Drover Cycles, Hay-on-Wye). [3] [5]

rows.label: Bike wash
rows.html: Yes — bike-wash area with a hose. [3]

rows.label: Food & toilets
rows.html: Hot drinks & snacks on site; "Farmer Mark Eats" hot food on weekends/bank holidays; toilet in the car park; sheltered picnic area. [3] [5]

rows.label: Camping / camper-van
rows.html: No on-site camping/campervans; nearby campsites in the Black Mountains / Brecon Beacons (e.g. Cwmdu, Pen-y-dre Farm) plus an on-farm holiday cottage (Airbnb). [3]

rows.label: Reputation / rating
rows.html: Very strong: TripAdvisor 4.9/5 (101 reviews); reviewers praise well-built jumps, friendly grass-roots vibe and good progression — noting it suits intermediate/advanced riders, not beginners. [7]
